title: ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config network not found in CNI cache
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I get this error message. ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config: network "appnet" not found in CNI cache 

appnet is the name of my custom network.  


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. run the command sudo podman container ls

2. if I run the same command again the error message doesn't appear.


**Describe the results you received:**
I received the list of containers after the error message

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected the result without the error message.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.8.0
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
Hi @pik10, thanks for opening this issue!

That's an error in ocicni (https://github.com/cri-o/ocicni/blob/master/pkg/ocicni/ocicni.go#L504-L508) and I would be prefer this to be a debug log; error logging a fall back isn't necessary IMHO.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan, can we cut a new release of ocicni to pull in the changes?

Sure, but I am not sure I have the power.
@haircommander @mrunalp PTAL

I honestly can't find evidence of there being rigorous versions of ocicni. the latest tag is 0.1.0, somehow the currently vendored version here is `github.com/cri-o/ocicni v0.1.1-0.20190920040751-deac903fd99b` I can't find where in the package the version is defined. Would you be opposed to pulling in the most recent commit instead @vrothberg

> Would you be opposed to pulling in the most recent commit instead @vrothberg

I don't feel strongly about it (given it would fix a bug) but it's a project under the CRI-O umbrella, so I have the expectation that it's doing a proper release management. It would make the lives of downstream packagers (e.g., Debian) much easier.
TBH I think it should live as a libpod package, but that conversation has dwindled
Might get yelled at, but probably ignored.
